message:
test(fuzz): tighten pattern-engine invariants and FuzzRuleDef shape

pattern_matcher_fuzz
  - Strengthen `assert_spans_within_bounds`: previously the check
    permitted a slack "engine may normalize whitespace" escape, which
    hid real span/text drift bugs where matched_text could exceed the
    source slice. Now assert:
      * matched_text.len() <= slice.len() — engines cannot introduce
        bytes that aren't in the source
      * matched_text equals slice OR is trim-equivalent OR is contained
        verbatim within the slice — any other shape is a drift bug
    Updated comment explains the invariant so future fuzz-target edits
    don't silently loosen it back.

pattern_pack_roundtrip
  - FuzzRuleDef's regex/remediation/workflow had `#[serde(default,
    skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`, but the real RuleDef
    carries plain `Option<String>` with no skip_serializing_if. A
    fuzzer input that's a fixed-point of FuzzRuleDef was therefore
    potentially NOT a fixed-point of RuleDef (serialization would
    differ by presence/absence of null keys), which silently let the
    fuzz target accept pack shapes the real loader would reject.
    Drop the skip attrs on those three fields; add a comment pinning
    the real RuleDef as source of truth.

No production code change. Pure fuzz-target hardening so future runs
surface the bug classes the loosened shape was masking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -44,12 +44,13 @@ struct FuzzRuleDef {
     event_type: String,
     severity: FuzzSeverity,
     anchors: Vec<String>,
-    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    // Real RuleDef: regex, remediation, workflow are plain Option<String>
+    // (no skip_serializing_if). Keep the mirror in lockstep — adding a skip
+    // attr here would make the fuzz target pass on a shape the real type
+    // doesn't accept as fixed-point.
     regex: Option<String>,
     description: String,
-    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
     remediation: Option<String>,
-    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
     workflow: Option<String>,
     #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
     manual_fix: Option<String>,
